content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining `ralph-burning-9ni.2.5` amendment for unrecoverable run failures. The milestone service now supports completion-time dispositions so ordinary bead failures still reconcile to retryable states while terminal project-run syncs can atomically mark the milestone `Failed` alongside the bead failure journal update. I also taught lifecycle repair to synthesize a `paused -> running -> failed` bridge when correcting older paused snapshots, and updated the CLI sync path plus regression coverage so failed run syncs now leave milestones in `Failed` with the required `running -> failed` journal event.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added completion disposition handling in the milestone service so task completion and repair flows can explicitly promote a reconciled snapshot to milestone `Failed` without changing the default retryable bead-failure behavior.
2. [Completed] Updated lifecycle reconciliation and bridge-event generation to support repaired terminal failure paths, including paused-snapshot correction into a valid running-to-failed chain with consistent transition reasons.
3. [Completed] Wired the CLI milestone sync path to use the new unrecoverable-failure disposition for failed project runs and expanded unit coverage for direct service behavior plus end-to-end sync assertions.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --all` completed.
- `cargo test unrecoverable_completion_marks_milestone_failed_and_emits_failed_transition --lib` passed.
- `cargo test repairing_failed_completion_can_promote_paused_snapshot_to_failed --lib` passed.
- `cargo test sync_terminal_milestone_task_marks_failed_runs_terminal --lib` passed.
- `cargo test milestone_record::service --lib` passed (76 tests).
- `cargo test cli::run::tests:: --lib` passed (13 tests).

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
